text: Handling deadlines effectively is an important skill in both work and study. Good time management helps you stay on track, so it helps to break a large project into smaller tasks and set personal deadlines. Prioritization is also key—focus on the most urgent or important tasks first. Sometimes you may need to manage the scope of a project, which means deciding what is realistic to finish within the time you have. If you feel overwhelmed, communication is crucial. Letting your manager, teacher, or teammates know early can lead to helpful adjustments or support. By planning ahead and staying open about challenges, you can meet deadlines with less stress.
